description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular box shape and a polished, reddish-brown wood exterior. The front panel features a woven speaker grille, two large white tuning knobs, and a horizontal frequency display with gold and white markings. Below the display, there are several push buttons for station selection, and above the grille, a metallic emblem or logo is visible. The sides of the radio have vertical slatted vents, likely for sound or heat dissipation. The back panel is made of a reddish-brown material with ventilation holes, technical markings, and various input/output ports, indicating its electrical functionality.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a woven speaker grille, tuning knobs, and a frequency display, set in a polished rectangular wooden case.
description_view_0: The front view shows the woven speaker grille, two large white knobs, a frequency display, push buttons, and a metallic emblem above the grille.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the polished wooden surface and a vertical slatted vent near the back.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a reddish-brown back panel with ventilation holes, technical markings, and various ports for electrical connections.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wooden surface and a matching vertical slatted vent.
